observations.recommendation: We have  recommended and Management agreed to henceforth, prepare the 1) Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances for In-kind Donations; 2) Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations; 3) Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of Donated Relief Goods; and 4) Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of Donated PPE; and furnish the same to the NDRRMC, through the OCD, copy furnished the Audit Team within ten  working days after the end of each quarter pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2014-002 and 2020-009.
observations.managementAction: The reports on donations were prepared by the receiving office.
observations.reason: During the exit conference, Management decided that the consolidation of the reports on donations shall be done by the General Services Officer for him to be aware of all the donations received by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to henceforth, conduct the mandatory capacity building program on GAD for the members of the GFPS Executive Committee and TWG, including elected officials and department heads, pursuant to Item 4.1.A.3 of JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2021, the Municipality budgeted the amount of ₱500,000.00 to fund the capability development of the LGU GAD Team but unable to utilize the same due to restricted movement of people brought by the COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: Restricted movements due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to maintain a special account for the BTC Shared Services Facilities through the use of complete subsidiary ledgers which will form part of the schedules of the General Fund at the end of the year.
observations.managementAction: Management informed that the BTC has undergone renovation and that they are planning to either rent it out to lessees, or become an office space for the LGU, this calendar year 2022.
observations.reason: Management plans to either rent out or convert the BTC into an office space. They will inform the Audit Team of the final decision this CY 2022.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 on the proper use of government vehicles and the General Services Officer to properly marked with the words “For Official Use Only” (3 inches) the agency’s owned vehicles, under which should be written the name of the Municipality of Bauko, Mountain Province, to prevent unauthorized use or improper use of the vehicles.

We have recommended and Management agreed to discontinue charging non-integrated services under the Aid to Individuals in Crisis Situation (AICS) program by revisiting its Expanded Medical AICS guidelines to include only the integrated services provided in Item VI.1 of the DSWD MC No.11;
We have recommended further and Management agreed to formulate a guideline on the implementation of its Regular Aid to Individuals in Crisis Situation program to provide for the specific program description and coverage, documentary requirements, and implementing procedures, among others.

We have recommended and Management agreed to include the frequency of availment in the provision of assistance in the Implementing Guidelines of the Expanded Medical Aid to Individuals in Crisis Situation program to avoid ambiguity to the client/beneficiary and the Municipal Social Worker and Development Office in charge of the screening and assessment and to increase the number of beneficiaries who will avail of the program.

We have recommended and Management agreed to surrender the next offspring of the cattle after weaning to the Provincial Veterinary Office and secure a certification from the Municipal Agriculturist and Provincial Veterinarian, in order to be relieved from responsibility and gain ownership of the remaining animals.
We have recommended further and Management agreed to make representation with the DA-RFO-CAR and the Provincial Veterinarian relative to the offspring of cow bearing PN 186 if these could be replaced since they are on now on the breeding stage while the requirement of the MOA is only a calf.

We have recommended and Management agreed to:
inform the National Dairy Authority of the sold offspring and secure their written approval, if warranted, to avoid repossession of the animals;
in the succeeding sale of offspring, the LGU shall secure prior written approval of the NDA and offer to the NDA the offspring should the LGU opt to sell or otherwise dispose of the same, in accordance with Item 4.2.4 of the Memorandum of Agreement on Dairy Animal Distribution Program; and
make representation with the NDA for the copies of Memoranda of Agreement covering the two batches of cattle deliveries; and the status of these dairy animals which have not been paid by the LGU and remain the property of NDA.

We have recommended and Management agreed to include the projects funded from the financial assistance to LGUs charged against the NDRRMF and LGSF-DRRAP in its 2022 Supplemental Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan and Supplemental Annual Investment Program, in accordance with the pertinent provisions of DBM Budget Circular Nos. 130 and 134.

We have recommended and Management agreed to reclassify the unutilized cash donation of ₱10,606.45 from the Other Payables account to the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account in the Trust Fund books.
We have recommended further and Management agreed to account succeeding cash donations for disaster relief aid, if any, under the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account in the Trust Fund books, and maintain separate subsidiary ledger for Trust Liabilities-DRRMF for cash donations received amounting to ₱100,000.00 and above per donor per purpose while those below ₱100,000.00 shall be grouped and posted in one SL labeled as Various Donors per purpose.

We have recommended and Management agreed to finalize its Revenue Code and submit the same to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan for review, pursuant to the pertinent provisions of RA No. 7160, in order to maximize the local revenue generation of the LGU.

We have recommended and Management agreed to strictly comply with the provisions of Presidential Decree No. 1445 in the processing of claims against government funds; and submit the photo documentations on the provision of Field and Laboratory Offices and the list of equipment, furniture, instruments, apparatus, affixtures, fittings and other items provided by the Contractor for said offices, to support the payment of engineering facilities, otherwise, the amount of ₱51,537.47 shall be disallowed in audit.

We have recommended and Management agreed to strictly comply with the pertinent provision of the General Appropriations Act on the mandatory minimum take home pay of employees which shall not be lower than Five Thousand Pesos (₱5,000.00).

We have recommended and Management agreed to:
require the contractors to immediately complete the quantity deficiencies in projects P-2020-06, INREMP-CAR-Bauko-01 and P-2019-03 amounted to ₱158,446.36 in order to conform with the approved plans and specifications;
compute and impose the corresponding liquidated damages on projects P-2019-09 and P-2020-03; and
require the contractor of project P-2019-03 to immediately remove and replace the defective plaster finish at his own expense.

We have recommended and Management agreed to require the contractor to immediately rectify/repair the deficiencies noted on the storage facility’s cement plaster finishing, plumbing fixtures and ordinary window glass to prevent early destruction of the project, and to avoid disallowance in audit.
We commended Management for diligently preparing the GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report as required under the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01.

We have recommended and Management agreed to:
present documents as evidence on the alleged partial settlement of ND No. 96-006-101 amounted to ₱37,384.00, otherwise, revert the amount in the Receivables-Disallowances and Charges account; and
notify in writing all persons identified to be liable for audit disallowances amounting to ₱456,489.91, prior to the effectivity of the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts, to settle their obligations.

We have recommended and Management agreed to create a special account in the General Fund for the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities for easy monitoring/tracking of the utilization of the allocation, pursuant to Section 3.7 of Local Budget Circular No. 125.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to submit the lacking documents and ensure that the required documents for emergency procurements (negotiated) are complete before payments are made in accordance with the provisions of the GPPB Circular No. 01-2020, to avoid disallowance; and to ensure that the Omnibus Sworn Statement is in accordance with the latest format prescribed in the same Circular.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the lacking documents and ensured that the required documents for ensuing emergency procurements (negotiated) were completed before payments are made.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to henceforth, ensure that the date of acceptance by the supplier in the conforme portion of the Purchase Order is indicated as basis for the imposition of penalty once the supplier failed to deliver it obligations.
observations.managementAction: Management ensured that the date of acceptance by the supplier in the conforme portion of the Purchase Order   is indicated.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to ensure that all the procedures under negotiated procurement (emergency cases) as required under GPPB Circular 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 are undertaken and the required documents are prepared to prevent abuses and corruption in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Management ensured that all the procedures under negotiated procurement (emergency cases) were undertaken and the required documents were prepared.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to henceforth, include a separate caption in the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Investment Plan or prepare a Supplemental MDRRMFIP to cover the Projects/Programs/Activities that will be charged against the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF before utilization of the same pursuant to Item 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Management prepared the supplemental MDRRMF IP funded from the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to terminate the time deposit account and transfer the principal amount plus interest of ₱2,962,015.82 to the Cash in Bank-LCCA in the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: Management terminated the time deposit account and transferred the principal amount plus interest to the Land Bank of the Philippines Local Currency Current Account-General Fund account of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to transfer its Cash in Bank account from the Philippine National Bank to the Land Bank of the Philippines in accordance with Items 5.2 and 6.1 of the Department of Finance Department Circular Nos. 001.2015 and 003.2015, respectively.
observations.managementAction: Management transferred the time deposit from the Philippine National Bank to the Land Bank of the Philippines Local Currency Current Account-General Fund account of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to turn over the delivery truck to the Proponent Group and ensure that this is utilized exclusively for the purpose of carrying out the objective of the project pursuant to Section 5.07.c of the Implementation Management Agreement between the Department of Agriculture and Municipal Local Government of Bauko, and pertinent provisions of PRDP Operations Manual I-REAP Component.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor turned over the delivery truck to the Proponent Group on February 18, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to submit the lacking documents to support the release of wages of eight beneficiaries to their representatives pursuant to Section 7(B) under the TUPAD Program of DOLE DO No. 173, series of 2017.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the lacking documents to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to strictly prepare the TUPAD payroll in accordance with the format prescribed in Annex I of DOLE Department Order No. 173, series of 2017.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to henceforth, refrain from recording the equipment purchased funded by the DOLE Kabuhayan Program as donation upon release to the beneficiaries pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009 and Section 12.D.3 of DOLE Department Order No. 173, series of 2017.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to require the project engineers to strictly monitor the implementation of the infrastructure projects to ensure that these are satisfactorily completed within the specified target completion dates in order for its constituents to enjoy the timely benefits that could be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and Management agreed to prepare and submit immediately to the Auditor the GAD Accomplishment Report for Calendar Year 2020, following the prescribed format pursuant to Items 4.1.A.4.g and 4.1.C.8.1 of the PCW, DBM, DILG and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We have  recommended further and Management agreed that the GAD AR as prescribed in Joint Memorandum Circular be accompanied by the following: (1) brief summary of the reported program or project; (2) copies of reported policy issuances; (3) results of HGDG tests; and (4) actions taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and recommendations, if any.
observations.managementAction:
Management prepared and submitted to the Auditor the GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2020 following the prescribed format.



The supporting documents were submitted by Management.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management require the parties to renew their pre marketing agreements to ensure the marketability of potato tubers in compliance with the PRDP’s duly-approved Business Plan.
observations.managementAction: The Proponent Group and the potato farm buyers executed their marketing agreements.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to henceforth, require business owners and organizations to submit certified photocopies of their ITRs in compliance with Section 1.e, Article A, Chapter III of the Updated Revenue Code for calendar year 2012 of the Municipality
observations.managementAction: Management required business owners and organizations to submit certified photocopies of their ITRs.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to henceforth, implement the planned IECs and the procurement of safety gears which were earlier identified to be of utmost priority in SWM.
observations.managementAction:
IECs are being conducted during People's Day, General Assemblies in the barangays and other activities.

The LGU procured safety gears this calendar year 2021.
observations.reason: The Municipality has prepared its 10-year SWM Plan (2019-2028), wherein for calendar years 2019-2021, the identified PPAs on the conduct of IECs and provision of IEC materials were not implemented. Budgeted amounts for calendar year 2021 amounted to ₱100,000.00, and ₱10,000.00, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to require the persons determined liable to settle the subject Notices of Disallowance in accordance with Section 10.4 of the COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: Management caused the persons liable in the disallowances totaling ₱1,445,296.10   to file their appeal to the COA-CAR Regional Office.
observations.reason: All the 36 NDs amounted to ₱1,445,296.10   were on appeal at the COA-CAR Regional Office.  On the NDs amounting to ₱456,489.91, the Management and the Audit Team do not have copies of the NDs and its supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to update its Revenue Code pursuant to Section 191 of RA No. 7160 to facilitate the increase in its local revenue generation.
observations.managementAction: For compliance by Management, to be spearheaded by the Sangguniang Bayan.
observations.reason: The updating of the Revenue Code was delayed because Management prioritized the immediate response to COVID-19 related endeavors.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to cause the refund of the fiscal year 2015 PEI granted to LGU officials and employees.
observations.managementAction: Management caused the full refund of the FY 2015 PEI as of December 31, 2020.
